info.name: Meta Creator Marketplace & Partnership Ads API
info.description: A comprehensive collection of API calls for the Meta Creator Marketplace and Partnership Ads integration. Includes endpoints for Creator Discovery (Instagram & Facebook), Permissions Management, Advertisable Media Management, Ad Creation, and Campaign Reporting & Insights. Enriched with real-world examples from the Partnership Ads Booster open-source reference application (github.com/honz/marketing-api-recipes).

request.description: Retrieve a list of Facebook Pages the user manages, along with their Page Access Tokens. This is the first step in the authentication flow.
Get the Instagram Professional Account ID linked to a specific Facebook Page.
Search for Instagram creators using filters like country, interests, and follower count.

response.name: Sample Response

item.name: Instagram Creator Marketplace - Get Reels & Insights

request.description: Get detailed insights, recent media, and branded content media for a specific Instagram creator.

response.name: Sample Response

request.description: Search for Facebook creators using semantic query and filters.

response.name: Sample Response

request.description: Get top performing content (e.g., Reels) for a specific Facebook creator.

item.name: Request Account-Level Permission (by Creator IG ID)

request.description: Send a request to a creator for account-level partnership ad permissions using the creator's Instagram account ID.

Alternative to requesting by ID: send a permission request using the creator's Instagram username instead. Useful when you only have the username from the discovery step. Source: partnership_ads_booster.py.

View the status of all account-level permission requests for the brand. Returns creator_ig_id, creator_username, and permission_status for each creator. Supports cursor-based pagination via paging.next. Source: partnership_ads_booster.py.
Revoke a previously granted account-level partnership ad permission from a creator. Pass revoke=true along with the creator_instagram_account ID.

item.name: 4. Advertisable Media Management
item.description: Endpoints for fetching and managing advertisable media from creators. These calls are derived from the Partnership Ads Booster reference application (partnership_ads_booster.py). They cover the full range of ways to query the branded_content_advertisable_medias edge.

request.description: Fetch all advertisable medias for the brand's Instagram account. Supports cursor-based pagination — use the paging.next URL for subsequent pages. The API maximum page size is 25. Source: partnership_ads_booster.py (fetch_all_advertisable_medias / fetch_page_of_advertisable_medias).

item.name: Fetch Advertisable Media (Filtered by Creator Username)

request.description: Fetch advertisable medias filtered by a specific creator's Instagram username. Highly recommended to avoid fetching large datasets when you already know which creator you want to boost. Source: partnership_ads_booster.py.

item.name: Check Media Eligibility by Ad Code (Shortcode)

request.description: Check the eligibility of a specific media using its Instagram shortcode (ad_code). The shortcode is the alphanumeric string extracted from the Instagram reel URL, e.g., 'C_Tlbb-sGMi' from 'https://www.instagram.com/reel/C_Tlbb-sGMi/'. Source: partnership_ads_booster.py (fetch_branded_content_advertisable_medias).

Check the eligibility of one or more media items by passing an array of Instagram shortcodes as the permalinks parameter. Useful when you have a list of posts from a CSV or UI input. Source: partnership_ads_booster.py.

Fetch basic engagement metrics (like_count and comments_count) for a specific Instagram media object. This is used in the Partnership Ads Booster to enrich the advertisable media list with engagement data before deciding which posts to boost. Source: partnership_ads_booster.py (fetch_media_insights).

item.name: Upload Instagram Video for Partnership Ad

request.description: Upload an Instagram video to the ad account's video library. This is a required step before creating the ad creative when using the permalink flow (as opposed to the ad_code flow). The returned video ID is then passed to the adcreatives endpoint. Source: partnership_ads_booster.py (upload_instagram_video).

item.name: Upload Instagram Video with Ad Code (Partnership Ad)

request.description: Upload an Instagram video using the ad_code (shortcode) flow. Pass partnership_ad_ad_code and is_partnership_ad=true in addition to source_instagram_media_id. This is the preferred flow when the creator has shared an ad code directly. Source: partnership_ads_booster.py (upload_instagram_video with ad_code).

item.name: Create Ad Creative - Boost Existing Post

request.description: Create an ad creative by boosting an existing Instagram post from a creator.

item.name: Create Ad Creative - With CTA, UTM & Testimonial (Full Options)

request.description: Create a partnership ad creative using the ad_code (instagram_boost_post_access_token) flow, with a call-to-action (CTA), UTM tracking parameters, and an optional testimonial overlay. This is the most complete creative creation call. Source: partnership_ads_booster.py (create_ad_creative with all optional parameters).

item.name: Create Ad Creative - With Advantage+ Catalog (Product Set)

request.description: Create a partnership ad creative that links to an Advantage+ Catalog product set. This enables dynamic product overlays on the creator's reel, driving direct product discovery and purchase. Requires degrees_of_freedom_spec with product_extensions OPT_IN and a creative_sourcing_spec with the product_set_id. Source: partnership_ads_booster.py (create_ad_creative with product_set_id).
Create a partnership ad using new creative assets (image/video) uploaded by the advertiser.

Create the final ad object with optional app events tracking_specs. When app_id is provided, the ad will track custom app events for mobile app campaigns. The ad is created in PAUSED status — change to ACTIVE when ready to go live. Source: partnership_ads_booster.py (create_ad with app_id).
Create and publish the ad using the generated creative_id. The ad starts in PAUSED status.

item.name: 6. Campaign Reporting & Insights
item.description: Endpoints for retrieving ad performance data, Reels-specific insights, and Business Manager asset information. Derived from reels_performant_creative_dashboard.py and helpers.py in the reference application.

item.name: Ad Account Insights - Reels Placement Breakdown

request.description: Retrieve ad-level insights filtered specifically for Instagram Reels placements. This is the core call used in the Reels Performant Creative Dashboard to identify which creator partnership ads are performing best on Reels. Source: reels_performant_creative_dashboard.py.
List all ad accounts owned by a Business Manager. Used to enumerate available ad accounts before creating campaigns. Source: helpers.py (get_ad_accounts_for_business_id).

response.name: Sample Response

request.description: List all product catalogs owned by a Business Manager. Required when using Advantage+ Catalog features in partnership ad creatives (product_set_id). Source: helpers.py (get_catalogs_for_business_id).

List all Meta Pixel (Ads Pixel) assets for a Business Manager. Useful for verifying pixel health and match rates before launching partnership ad campaigns that rely on conversion tracking. Source: helpers.py (get_pixels_for_business_id).

Get detailed stats for a specific Meta Pixel, including match rate, event statistics, and automatic matching fields. High match rates (above 70%) are critical for effective conversion optimization in partnership ad campaigns. Source: helpers.py (get_stats_and_settings_for_pixels).
